MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar May-01-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Danielle Perez's motion to compel further responses to requests for admission is granted. As to requests #12 and 13, the objection pursuant to Evidence Code Section 1151 is overruled. The relevancy objection is also overruled. While Evidence Code Section 669.1 may prevent specified regulations from establishing a presumption of negligence, the authorities cited by defendant do not stand for the proposition that the subject matter of the requests are irrelevant. Both Lugtu v. California Highway Patrol (2001) 26 Cal. 4th 703 and Evidence Code Section 669.1 provide that defendant's rules may be considered by the trier of fact in determining negligence in a particular case. Accordingly, defendant is ordered to serve supplemental responses on or before May 5, 2006.

As to requests #14 - 20, the objection of vague is overruled. Defendant is ordered to serve supplemental responses on or before May 5, 2006. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
